steps.body: When only **conservative** forces do work, mechanical energy is conserved: **KE + U = constant**. With **friction**, mechanical energy decreases — the rest becomes **thermal energy (E_th)**.

steps.prompt: A **2 kg** block starts at rest at **h = 2 m** and slides down a **frictionless** ramp. Drag the **KE** at the bottom.
hints: No friction → U_g lost = KE gained.

steps.prompt: Same ramp (**h = 2 m**, **m = 2 kg**) but **μ_k = 0.25** over **4 m** of track. Drag **E_th** produced.
With friction, total mechanical energy drops. Starting **U_g = 39.2 J**, **E_th = 19.6 J** — drag the **KE** left at the bottom.
hints: Energy accounting: U_g,initial = KE + E_th (started from rest).

steps.title: Lesson complete!
steps.body: You balanced energy with and without friction — conservation when it applies, thermal energy when it doesn't.
